description: SpyTag — 13-aa peptide (AHIVMVDAYKPTK) from the CnaB2 domain of S. pyogenes FbaB that forms a spontaneous, irreversible isopeptide bond to its protein partner SpyCatcher.

references.authors: Zakeri B, Fierer JO, Celik E, Chittock EC, Schwarz-Linek U, Moy VT, Howarth M
references.title: Peptide tag forming a rapid covalent bond to a protein, through engineering a bacterial adhesin
references.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2012)

references.authors: Li L, Fierer JO, Rapoport TA, Howarth M
references.title: Structural analysis and optimization of the covalent association between SpyCatcher and a peptide Tag

references.authors: Keeble AH, Banerjee A, Ferla MP, Reddington SC, Anuar INAK, Howarth M
references.title: Evolving Accelerated Amidation by SpyTag/SpyCatcher to Analyze Membrane Dynamics

references.authors: Keeble AH, Turkki P, Stokes S, Khairil Anuar INA, Rahikainen R, Hytönen VP, Howarth M
references.title: Approaching infinite affinity through engineering of peptide-protein interaction
references.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2019)

references.authors: Hatlem D, Trunk T, Linke D, Leo JC
references.title: Catching a SPY: Using the SpyCatcher-SpyTag and Related Systems for Labeling and Localizing Bacterial Proteins

references.authors: Keeble AH, Yadav VK, Ferla MP, Bauer CC, Chuntharpursat-Bon E, Huang J, Bon RS, Howarth M
references.title: DogCatcher allows loop-friendly protein-protein ligation

label: SpyTag
note: 13-aa peptide tag (AHIVMVDAYKPTK) engineered by splitting the CnaB2 immunoglobulin-like domain of Streptococcus pyogenes fibronectin-binding protein FbaB (UniProt Q6A1F3, residues 550-562). On mixing with its 116-aa protein partner SpyCatcher, the tag's Asp7 spontaneously forms an intermolecular isopeptide (amide) bond to Lys31 of SpyCatcher, catalysed by SpyCatcher Glu77, yielding an irreversible covalent linkage. Fusable at N-terminus, C-terminus, or internally.

provenance.sequence_source: UniProt Q6A1F3 (FbaB / fibronectin-binding protein F2, Streptococcus pyogenes), positions 550-562, 100% identity over 13 aa; independently fetched 2026-06-20 via rest.uniprot.org and confirmed via NCBI efetch of backing record CAH04964.1

functional_claims.label: Spontaneous, irreversible isopeptide (amide) bond to SpyCatcher

value.reversibility: irreversible; resists boiling in SDS and competing peptide

source.quote: The electron density map clearly confirms the isopeptide bond between Lys31 of SpyCatcher and Asp7 of SpyTag
source.section: Crystal structure of the SpyTag/SpyCatcher complex

functional_claims.label: Reconstituted complex forms a beta-sandwich; only 10 of 13 SpyTag residues contact SpyCatcher

source.quote: only 10 out of 13 amino acids of the SpyTag peptide were observed to interact with SpyCatcher, pointing to specific contacts important for rapid split protein reconstitution

functional_claims.label: C-terminal Pro11-Thr12-Lys13 (PTK) tripeptide is dispensable for binding

value.evidence: Pro11/Thr12 contact only a neighbouring molecule in the crystal lattice; Lys13 is disordered

source.quote: our structures suggest that the three C-terminal amino acids of SpyTag peptide (PTK) may not have a direct role in stabilizing the initial non-covalent complex between SpyTag and SpyCatcher: Pro11 and Thr12 are visible, but these two amino acids interact with residues of the neighboring SpyCatcher molecule in the crystal, while Lys13 is disordered
source.section: Crystal structure of the SpyTag/SpyCatcher complex

functional_claims.label: Second-order reaction with SpyCatcher: k ~1.4e3 M-1 s-1, t1/2 ~74 s at 10 uM

value.reaction: covalent isopeptide bond formation with SpyCatcher

source.quote: The two partners then react rapidly, forming the isopeptide bond, with a half-time of 74 s for partners at 10 μM.

functional_claims.label: Fusable at N-terminus, C-terminus, or internally

source.quote: SpyTag could be fused at either terminus or internally and reacted specifically at the mammalian cell surface.

functional_claims.label: Reaction is robust across pH, temperature and buffer; bond resists boiling and force >1 nN

value.stability: not reversed by boiling or competing peptide

source.quote: Reaction occurred in high yield simply upon mixing and amidst diverse conditions of pH, temperature, and buffer... Single-molecule dynamic force spectroscopy showed that SpyTag did not separate from SpyCatcher until the force exceeded 1 nN, where covalent bonds snap.

functional_claims.label: Works in bacteria and at the mammalian cell surface

source.quote: SpyTag could be fused at either terminus or internally and reacted specifically at the mammalian cell surface.

functional_claims.label: Derived from the CnaB2 domain of S. pyogenes FbaB (UniProt Q6A1F3 residues 550-562)

value.design: split of CnaB2 into peptide (SpyTag) + protein (SpyCatcher) fragments with rational engineering

source.quote: fibronectin-binding protein FbaB contains a domain with a spontaneous isopeptide bond between Lys and Asp. By splitting this domain and rational engineering of the fragments, we obtained a peptide (SpyTag) which formed an amide bond to its protein partner (SpyCatcher)

variants.name: SpyTag003
variants.note: approaches the diffusion limit; pairs with SpyCatcher003

source.quote: The second-order rate constant for SpyTag003/SpyCatcher003 was 5.5 ± 0.6 × 10[5] M[-1]s[-1] ... ∼400-fold faster than the 1.4 × 10[3] M[-1]s[-1] previously shown for SpyTag/SpyCatcher

functional_claims.label: Genetically encoded covalent module for protein assembly, immobilization, vaccines and biomaterials

applications: stringent affinity capture via covalent linkage
